  "findings": [
    "Bearing inner ring exhibits bluing indicating thermal damage/overheating during induction or flame heating",
    "Metallurgical structure of the bearing steel has been compromised, reducing hardness and fatigue life"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject and remove the overheated bearing assembly",
    "Verify calibration of the induction heater or temperature-monitoring equipment",
    "Install a new bearing using proper temperature controls (maximum 120 degrees C / 250 degrees F)"
  ],
  "rationale": "The bearing shows clear signs of overheating (bluing of the inner ring) during the mounting process. According to SKF and Timken mounting guidelines, heating bearings above 120 degrees C (250 degrees F) alters the metallurgy, reduces hardness, and leads to premature bearing failure. The component is installed-defective and must be replaced before closeout.",
  "references": [
    "SKF Bearing Installation and Maintenance Guide",
    "Timken Bearing Damage Analysis Guide (Thermal Damage / Overheating)"
  ]
}
